title: Beijing World Championship
tagline: Creating a 10-year anniversary spectacle during a pandemic.

body: Due to the pandemic and the event's location in China, we faced numerous creative restrictions — strict limits on how many people could be on stage at once, creative partners unable to legally enter the country, and music performers unable to travel, among many other challenges.

The show had to deliver on multiple fronts: a full AR performance marking K/DA's return to the main stage, a celebration of the 10-year anniversary of League of Legends Esports, and a spotlight on the two teams competing in the championship match.

It was the 10th anniversary of the World Championship and Riot aimed to create the biggest celebration in company history. As Creative Director, I led a diverse team of external agencies, stage designers, lighting designers, and choreographers to craft an authentic tribute honoring the tournament's legacy — the first live event returning from the pandemic.

Adapting the creative vision in the months leading up to the show while staying true to the core pillars was critical. We delivered a 15-minute experience featuring a 30-foot-tall set design, choreography seamlessly integrated with content, stunning lighting visuals, and massive AR activations that transformed the entire stadium.
led: Led external agencies and design teams
Adapted the vision around pandemic constraints

impact.note: The show was a massive success, garnering Riot Games their third Emmy Award — viewed live by 100M+ and earning critical acclaim across social channels.
